quality_tier.title: Australian Government International Scholarship 2027
quality_tier.company: supporting education and skills development worldwide
quality_tier.description_short: The Australian Government, through its Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), is committed to building strong global partnerships by supporting education and skills development worldwide. DFA
quality_tier.description_full: The Australian Government, through its Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), is committed to building strong global partnerships by supporting education and skills development worldwide. DFAT works to promote Australia’s international interests while contributing to sustainable development, economic growth, and long-term cooperation between countries. Through its scholarship programmes, the Australian Government provides international students with the opportunity to study at leading Australian institutions, gain valuable skills, and contribute positively to their home countries and communities after graduation. Australia Awards Scholarships are fully funded, long-term study opportunities supported by the Australian Government through the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. These scholarships are designed to help students from developing countries, especially those in the Indo-Pacific region, gain valuable education and research experience in Australia. The Australian Government is inviting students to apply for their international scholarship programme, for the . Scholarships will be awarded to students from developing countries, for . The scholarship is open to students from the following countries: The fields of study differ by country – Africa’s priority fields of study include: www.zabursaries.co.za is committed to keeping you up to date with the latest bursary opportunities, and this article covers all details about the Australian Government International Scholarship. Australia Awards Scholarships cover the full length of your approved study programme at an Australian university. This includes any required foundation or preparatory courses you need before starting your main qualification. The scholarship will provide cover for the following expenses: Scholars will be required to sign a contract with the Commonwealth of Australia declaring that they will comply with the conditions of the scholarship. Applicants must satisfy the following minimum entry criteria before applying (please note that failure to satisfy all the requirements will lead to your application not being considered): Applications must be submitted online at: Read the before applying. Applicants should download and read the following: 30 April 2026 (at 14:00 AEST). (Applications submitted after this date will not be accepted – please apply before the closing date to ensure your application is considered.) For any queries related to this bursary programme, please contact the Australian Government directly: African students: Email: enquiries@australiaawardsafrica.org For students from Asian and Pacific countries, find the email address for your country here: https://www.dfat.gov.au/people-to-people/australia-awards/participating-countries Please do NOT contact the Australian Government if this bursary has already closed. quality_tier.title: Allan Gray Orbis Fellowship Bursary South Africa 2027
quality_tier.description_short: The Allan Gray Orbis Foundation is inviting students to apply for their Fellowship Programme, for the . The fellowship (bursary) will be awarded for within these fields, at selected institutions (WITS
quality_tier.description_full: The Allan Gray Orbis Foundation is inviting students to apply for their Fellowship Programme, for the . The fellowship (bursary) will be awarded for within these fields, at selected institutions (WITS, UJ, UCT, NMU, RU, UWC, SU, UP, UFS, UKZN or TSiBA): The Fellowship Programme aims to help students by cultivating entrepreneurship in Fellows, and ensuring that the Programme results in learners seeking to create entrepreneurship opportunities. Successful candidates will be boarded as Candidate Allan Gray Fellows. The value of the bursary will depend on the students household income: Students will benefit from a range of entrepreneurial support services, including personalized mentorship from a dedicated foundation staff member and an experienced business mentor. They will also gain access to the foundation’s exclusive online entrepreneurial curriculum, participate in an annual entrepreneurship conference, and attend specialized seminars and workshops. Additionally, students will be part of a vibrant community of like-minded entrepreneurs, have opportunities for business incubation, and explore funding avenues through E Squared, a sister entity of the Allan Gray Orbis Foundation. The selection process will include the following steps and dates: Step 1: applications must be submitted by no later than 30 April 2026 at 17:00. Step 2: shortlisted applicants will be contacted and invited to attend an interview on 31 July 2026. Step 3: shortlisted applicants will be invited to attend a Selection Camp in September 2026. All applicants will receive feedback after every step of the selection process, to keep them updated on the status of their application. Applicants must satisfy the following minimum entry criteria before applying (please note that failure to satisfy all the requirements will lead to your application not being considered): Applications must be submitted in of the following ways: Submit clear copies of the following supporting documentation along with your completed application form (the submission of these documents is compulsory – if any items are missing, your application may be disqualified): 30 April 2026 (at 17:00). (Applications submitted after these dates will not be accepted – please apply before the closing date to ensure your application is considered.) For any queries related to this bursary programme, please contact the Allan Gray Orbis Foundation directly: Please do NOT contact the Allan Gray Orbis Foundation if this bursary has already closed. View our other bursaries open for application here: The Allan Gray Orbis Foundation was founded by Allan Gray in 2005 out of his dream to create a sustainable contribution to the country, by helping students who are the future entrepreneurs of Southern Africa. The Foundation receives its funding from the donation of Allan Gray Limited’s taxed profits. The Foundation currently operates in 4 countries across Africa, namely South Africa; Namibia; Swaziland and Botswana. The Allan Gray Orbis Foundation Fellowship programme aims to provide funding for students to study at university level, as well as providing support and development for those who demonstrate an entrepreneurial mindset.

quality_tier.title: Arts & Culture Trust (ACT) Nyoloha Scholarship South Africa 2027
quality_tier.description_short: The Arts & Culture Trust (ACT), partnered with Nedbank Arts Affinity, supported by Nedbank, and MTN Foundation South Africa, is inviting students to apply for the . Scholarships will be awarded for un
quality_tier.description_full: The Arts & Culture Trust (ACT), partnered with Nedbank Arts Affinity, supported by Nedbank, and MTN Foundation South Africa, is inviting students to apply for the . Scholarships will be awarded for undergraduate studies within the following fields: www.zabursaries.co.za is committed to keeping you up to date with the latest bursary opportunities, and this article covers all details about the ACT Nyoloha Scholarship. 2 full scholarships will be awarded, up to a maximum value of R300 000, for studies at a tertiary or an accredited South African arts institution. Scholarships will cover registration fees, tuition fees, and study materials (up to R5 000 per year). It will NOT cover accommodation, transport, or living expenses. Selected applicants will be contacted by the end of May 2026 and unsuccessful applicants will be notified accordingly. Applicants must satisfy the following minimum entry criteria before applying (please note that failure to satisfy all the requirements will lead to your application not being considered): Students may apply by following these steps: Complete the online: Applicants should read the (.pdf) before applying. Upload clear copies of the following supporting documentation along with your completed online application form (the submission of these documents is compulsory – if any items are missing, your application may be disqualified): (Applications submitted after this date will not be accepted – please apply before the closing date to ensure your application is considered.) For any queries related to this scholarship programme, please contact the Arts & Culture Trust directly: Tel: 010 023 0899 Email: info@act.org.za The Arts & Culture Trust (ACT) opened its doors in 1994, when it was known as the Arts and Culture Trust of the President. 28 years later, ACT serves the arts and culture sector in all 9 provinces of South Africa, as a registered non-profit organisation and is completely independent of government. The Arts & Culture Trust (ACT) Nyoloha Scholarship Programme (NSP) was formed as a partnership between ACT and Nedbank Arts Affinity, with the goal of funding higher education for young South African performers and visual artists.

quality_tier.title: Afrimat Mining Services Bursary South Africa 2027
quality_tier.description_short: Afrimat Mining Services, is inviting students to apply for their bursary programme for the . Bursaries will be awarded for studies within the field. Afrimat is seeking to support talented and motivate
quality_tier.description_full: Afrimat Mining Services, is inviting students to apply for their bursary programme for the . Bursaries will be awarded for studies within the field. Afrimat is seeking to support talented and motivated students, with a passion for the industry. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted – if you do not receive any feedback, please regard your application as unsuccessful. Applicants must satisfy the following minimum entry criteria before applying (please note that failure to satisfy all the requirements will lead to your application not being considered): Applications must be submitted online at: Submit clear copies of the following supporting documentation along with your application (the submission of these documents is compulsory – if any items are missing, your application may be disqualified): (Applications submitted after this date will not be accepted – please apply before the closing date to ensure your application is considered.) For any queries related to this bursary programme, please contact Afrimat directly: Afrimat Limited is a mining company and provider of construction materials and industrial minerals to the construction, infrastructure, building and civil markets in South Africa (particularly in Gauteng, the Western Cape, KwaZulu-Natal, the Eastern Cape, the Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, the Free State and Limpopo) and Mozambique. The company has 5 main divisions, namely: Readymix, Contracting International, Concrete Products, Mining & Aggregates and Industrial Minerals. The group service projects of various scales – from small-scale private sector contracts, to large-scale construction and infrastructure projects for parastatals and state-owned enterprises. Afrimat’s main mission is to operate opencast mines, provide customers with contracting services and to add value via mined products. The leading black empowered company has been listed on the JSE Limited since 2006, under the Construction & Building Materials section and is backed by over 50 years of experience. The company prides itself on low staff turnover, which has lead to a large skills pool.

quality_tier.title: Afrimat Denver Quarry Bursary South Africa 2027
quality_tier.description_short: Afrimat CM Eastern Cape (Pty) Ltd – Denver Quarry, is inviting students to apply for their bursary programme for the . Bursaries will be awarded to students residing in communities within the (Eastern
quality_tier.description_full: Afrimat CM Eastern Cape (Pty) Ltd – Denver Quarry, is inviting students to apply for their bursary programme for the . Bursaries will be awarded to students residing in communities within the (Eastern Cape), for studies within the following fields: Afrimat is seeking to support talented and motivated students, with a passion for the industry. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted – if you do not receive any feedback, please regard your application as unsuccessful. Applicants must satisfy the following minimum entry criteria before applying (please note that failure to satisfy all the requirements will lead to your application not being considered): Applications must be submitted online at: Submit clear copies of the following supporting documentation along with your application (the submission of these documents is compulsory – if any items are missing, your application may be disqualified): (Applications submitted after this date will not be accepted – please apply before the closing date to ensure your application is considered.) For any queries related to this bursary programme, please contact Afrimat directly: Afrimat is a leading mid-tier mining and materials company that plays a vital role in supporting South Africa’s construction and infrastructure sectors. With a strong national footprint, the company supplies aggregates, concrete products, and industrial minerals, built on a reputation for reliability, efficiency, and sustainable practices. Among its key operations is the Denver Quarry, located in Johannesburg, which contributes significantly to the production of high-quality crushed stone for urban development and road construction. The quarry operates with a focus on consistent output, safety, and environmental responsibility, helping meet the growing demand for durable construction materials. Together, Afrimat and its Denver Quarry reflect a commitment to delivering essential resources that underpin economic growth and infrastructure development across the region.

raw_data.description_full: Sasol invites South African students to apply for the Mainstream Bursary Programme for the 2027 academic year. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Opening Date: 01 April 2026 Bursary Application Closing Date: 17 May 2026 Study Location: Recognised South African Universities Bursary Overview The Sasol Mainstream Bursary Programme 2027 offers a comprehensive funding package for students pursuing full-time university studies in engineering and science fields . The bursary includes: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Full financial support (tuition, accommodation, etc.) Developmental support and mentorship Possible employment through Sasol’s Graduate Development Programmes ⚠️ Note: This bursary includes a workback/payback obligation after completion of studies. Fields of Study Undergraduate (BEng / BSc) Applicants may study the following: Chemical Engineering Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Electronic Engineering Industrial Engineering Mechanical Engineering Mining Engineering Metallurgical Engineering Chemistry (up to Honours level required) Geology (up to Honours level required) Data Science (Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Operations Research) Undergraduate (BEng Tech) Mine Surveying (University of Technology only) Postgraduate (MSc / MEng / PhD) Fields include: Chemical Engineering (Fluid Dynamics, Thermodynamics, Process Modelling, etc.) Chemistry (Catalysis, Polymer Science, Environmental Science, Analytical Chemistry) Cyber Security Data Science (Applied Mathematics, Statistics) Minimum Requirements Applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be a South African citizen Be under 30 years of age Intend to study full-time at a recognised South African university Have strong academic results ⚠️ Important: Mathematics (not Maths Literacy) is required No distance learning (e.g., UNISA not accepted) Only degree programmes (no diplomas) Important Conditions Only one application per applicant is allowed Applications must be submitted online only Incorrect or incomplete information will result in disqualification Selection is highly competitive Bursaries are awarded based on academic performance and Sasol business needs Required Documents Applicants must ensure they submit: Academic results (Grade 11 or latest results) Certified ID copy Any additional required supporting documents How to Apply Click here to apply for Sasol Mainstream Bursary Programme 2027: For enquiries: Email: berlyne.rasool@adcorpgroup.com Tel: 086 010 6235 Submit your application online before the closing date. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Notes Applications must be submitted via the official Sasol bursary portal Email applications will not be accepted Only South African citizens may apply

raw_data.description_full: Flyfofa Aviation Training invites South African youth to apply for the Advanced Pilot Training (300-Hour Building) Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 11 April 2026 Bursary Location: Gauteng Province, South Africa Overview of the Flyfofa Aviation NSF Bursary Programme 2026 Flyfofa Aviation Training, in partnership with the National Skills Fund (NSF) and the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET), is offering an exciting opportunity for aspiring pilots to advance their careers through the Advanced Pilot Training (300-Hour Building) Programme . This bursary programme is designed to help licensed pilots overcome one of the biggest barriers in aviation — building sufficient flight hours required for employment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); If you already hold a Private Pilot Licence (PPL) or Commercial Pilot Licence (CPL) , this programme can help you transition from training into a professional aviation career. Bursary Programme Details Programme: Advanced Pilot Training (300 Flight Hours) Training Provider: Flyfofa Aviation Training Funding Partner: National Skills Fund (NSF) Location: Gauteng Province Application Code: PHB300 Minimum Qualification: PPL or CPL (SACAA recognised) Age Requirement: 35 years or younger Target Group: Previously disadvantaged individuals Closing Date: 11 April 2026 Why This Programme Matters Many pilots struggle to find employment after obtaining their licence due to insufficient flight hours . This programme directly addresses that challenge by offering: Structured flight hour accumulation Professional aviation exposure SACAA-aligned training progression Improved employability Completing an additional 300 flight hours significantly increases your chances of entering: Charter aviation companies Entry-level airline roles Flight instruction programmes Specialised aviation services Who Should Apply? This bursary is ideal for candidates who: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Already hold a PPL or CPL Need additional flight hours Cannot afford further training costs Want to enter commercial aviation Are committed to a long-term aviation career Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen Must be 35 years old or younger Must reside in Gauteng Province Must hold: Private Pilot Licence (PPL), or Commercial Pilot Licence (CPL) Must belong to previously disadvantaged groups (priority target) Documents Required Applicants must prepare: Copy of South African ID Cover letter (motivation) Proof of residence in Gauteng Copy of SACAA licence Last three copies of flight logbook Ensure all documents are clear and certified where required. How to Apply Online Application Click here to apply for Flyfofa Aviation NSF Bursary Programme – Advanced Pilot Training 2026 Email Application Alternatively, submit your application via email: Email: applications@flyfofa.co.za (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Subject Line: PHB300 Application – Advanced Hour Building Programme Attach the following: ID copy Cover letter Proof of residence SACAA licence copy Last three logbook copies This bursary programme is a rare opportunity for aspiring pilots to gain critical flight hours, reduce financial barriers, and move closer to a professional aviation career in South Africa.

raw_data.description_full: State Information Technology Agency (SITA) invites South African students to apply for the External Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 01 April 2026 Bursary Location: South Africa About the SITA External Bursary Programme 2026 The State Information Technology Agency (SITA) is offering a high-value bursary programme aimed at supporting students pursuing careers in Information and Communication Technology (ICT) . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This bursary is specifically targeted at second-year students who demonstrate strong academic performance and financial need. A total of 20 bursaries will be awarded nationwide. Fields Covered The bursary supports studies in ICT-related qualifications, including: BSc Computer Science BSc Computer Science and Informatics (Artificial Intelligence) BSc Information Technology BSc Data Science Diploma in ICT (Application Development) Diploma in ICT (Communication Networks) BSc Cybersecurity What the Bursary Covers The bursary provides comprehensive funding, including: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Tuition fees Prescribed textbooks Accommodation Meals Study allowance Work Integrated Learning (where applicable) Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet all of the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen Must be registered for second year of study Must study at a recognised public tertiary institution Must be studying an approved ICT qualification Must have a minimum academic average of 65% Must not have another bursary Must not have previously received a SITA bursary Preference may be given in line with Employment Equity and to students living with disabilities. Required Documents Applicants must submit: Certified copy of South African ID Certified copy of Matric results Proof of registration Acceptance letter from institution Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic transcript (first-year results) Incomplete applications may not be considered. How to Apply Click here to apply for SITA External Bursary Programme 2026: externalbursaries2026@sita.co.za Email address: externalbursaries2026@sita.co.za (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Applicants must: Complete the official SITA bursary application form Prepare all required documents Submit documents via email before the closing date Important Notes Only 20 bursaries will be awarded nationwide Late applications will not be accepted Only successful applicants will be contacted If you do not receive feedback within 3 months after the closing date , consider your application unsuccessful Selection is based on academic merit, financial need, and Employment Equity Career Opportunities After Completion Graduates funded through this programme may pursue careers such as: Software Developer Data Scientist Cybersecurity Analyst Network Engineer Systems Administrator ICT Specialist in government

raw_data.description_full: Tronox invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 31 March 2026 (at 16:00) Bursary Location: Empangeni and Mtunzini, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa About the Tronox Bursary Programme 2026 Tronox is offering bursaries to support students pursuing studies in key technical and science fields aligned with its operations in KwaZulu-Natal. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The bursary aims to develop young talent and contribute to community upliftment, particularly in areas surrounding Tronox KZN Sands operations. Fields of Study Covered Bursaries will be awarded for the following qualifications: Electrical Engineering (Heavy) (National Diploma / BSc / BEng) Geology (BSc) Information Technology (National Diploma / BSc) Selection Process Applicants may undergo: Psychometric assessments Background checks Medical examinations Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If you do not receive feedback within 4 weeks after the closing date , consider your application unsuccessful. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be a South African citizen Have completed Matric Be studying towards one of the listed fields Be in their 1st year of study in 2026 Not be currently or previously registered with any SETA learnership programme Preference will be given to students from: Kwa Dube Area (Inkosi Dube) Kwa Nzuza Area (Inkosi Nzuza) Kwa Mkhwanazi (Inkosi Mkhwanazi) Ogagwini (Inkosi Zulu) Somopho (Inkosi Mthembu) Macambini (Inkosi Mathaba) Madlebe (Inkosi Zungu) Or municipalities: Umhlathuze Umlalazi Mandeni Females and people living with disabilities are encouraged to apply. Required Documents Applicants must submit: Certified copy of ID (not older than 3 months) Certified Matric certificate or results Proof of registration or acceptance letter 1-page Curriculum Vitae (CV) Proof of residence from Traditional Council Incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Click here to apply for Tronox Bursary South Africa 2026: Recruitment.KZNSands@Tronox.com (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Email address: Recruitment.KZNSands@Tronox.com Email Subject Line: Tronox Bursary 2026 – Your Field of Study Important Notes No application form is required Applications must be submitted via email Late applications will not be accepted Ensure all documents are certified and complete

raw_data.description_full: Southern Sphere Platinum invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 6 April 2026 (at 17:00) Bursary Location: Sekhukhune District, Limpopo, South Africa About Southern Sphere Platinum Southern Sphere Platinum is a mining exploration and development company focusing on platinum group metals (PGMs) and chrome in the Steelpoort region. The company is committed to uplifting local communities by investing in education and skills development, especially for students pursuing careers in the mining and related industries. Fields Covered The bursary is available for students studying in the following fields: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Chemical Engineering Data Science Electrical Engineering Environmental Science / Environmental Management / Ecology / Conservation Biology Geology Information Technology / Computer Science Legal Mechanical Engineering Metallurgical Engineering Mining Engineering Process Engineering Bursary Benefits The bursary covers expenses up to R200 000 per student , including: Tuition fees Prescribed textbooks Accommodation (residence or approved housing) Meal allowance Note: The bursary does not cover outstanding fees from previous years. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen Must reside in Sekhukhune District (Limpopo) Must be studying in one of the listed fields Must be entering 2nd year of study in 2026 Must have a minimum average of 60% in 1st year Must be studying at one of these universities: University of Limpopo North-West University University of the Witwatersrand University of Johannesburg University of Pretoria Must not be receiving another bursary Preference given to students with financial need Required Documents Submit the following documents: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Motivational letter Certified copy of ID Matric certificate Full academic record (1st year) Proof of registration/acceptance Proof of residence in Sekhukhune District Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 21 days after the closing date Additional assessments (psychometric tests or interviews) may be required How to Apply Send your application via email: 📧 bursaries@southernsphere.co.za 📌 Use subject line: “Southern Sphere Platinum Bursary 2026”

raw_data.description_full: South African Road Federation invites students to apply for the SARF Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Closing Date: 31 August 2026 Funding Type: Partial (Supplementary Bursary) Study Level: Diploma, Degree, and Postgraduate Institutions: South African universities and Universities of Technology About the SARF Bursary Programme The SARF Bursary Programme is a strategic funding opportunity aimed at developing skills in South Africa’s road transport and infrastructure sector . This bursary supports students pursuing careers in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Road engineering Transport management Transport economics Road safety Unlike many bursaries, SARF strongly encourages postgraduate studies (MSc, MTech and beyond) , making it ideal for students aiming to become specialists in infrastructure and transport systems. Fields Covered Road Engineering (Civil) Traffic engineering Transportation planning Pavement and bridge engineering Construction materials and management Environmental and stormwater engineering Road Transport Management Logistics coordination Fleet operations Transport systems optimisation Freight planning Transport Economics Infrastructure investment analysis Transport policy development Cost-benefit modelling Freight corridor planning Road Safety Engineering Accident prevention systems Traffic flow design Smart road technologies Highway safety assessments Who Should Apply? You should apply if you are: Studying civil engineering (transport focus) Studying logistics or transport management Studying transport economics Studying road safety engineering Planning postgraduate studies in infrastructure Minimum Requirements Diploma Students: Must have passed Semester 1 Degree Students: Must have passed first year Postgraduate Students: Preference given to MSc, MTech, and advanced research students Institution Requirements Applicants must: Study at a recognised South African institution Be endorsed by their academic institution Proof of enrolment Academic performance Programme suitability Funding Coverage The SARF bursary is a supplementary bursary , meaning: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Amount varies per student Determined by SARF Council Paid directly to your institution Covers part of study costs Students are encouraged to combine funding with: NSFAS University bursaries SETA funding Employer sponsorship Selection Preference Preference is given to applicants who: Demonstrate strong academic performance Plan careers in the transport sector Work for SARF member organisations Show commitment to infrastructure development South African residents are prioritised, but SADC applicants may also apply . Important Requirement Applicants must show commitment to working in the South African road transport sector after completing their studies. Required Documents Prepare the following: Certified ID copy Academic transcripts Proof of registration CV Motivation letter Institutional endorsement letter How to Apply Request and submit your application form via: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Address: South African Road Federation 48 Gladiator Street Rhodesfield Kempton Park, 1619 Postal Address: PO Box 8379 Birchleigh, 1621 Contact: Tel: 011 394 9025 / 011 394 1459 Email: sybul@sarf.org.za Important Notes Closing date: 31 August 2026 Late applications are not accepted Ensure all documents are complete before submission

raw_data.description_full: ASSITEJ South Africa invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 29 April 2026 Internship Locations: Cape Town & Johannesburg, South Africa Internship Overview ASSITEJ South Africa is offering 5 internship opportunities for young people passionate about arts, youth development, and social impact . This programme is ideal for candidates who want to kick-start their careers while making a difference in the lives of children and young people through the arts . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Available Internship Positions 1. Finance Intern (X1) 📍 Cape Town Support financial data management Assist with reporting, budgeting, and analysis 2. M&E Data Capturer & Research Interns (X2) 📍 Cape Town (1) & Johannesburg (1) Assist with data capturing and M&E systems Conduct research and reporting Support impact evaluation 3. Administrative Assistant Intern (X1) 📍 Johannesburg Provide administrative support Assist with filing, data capture, and office management Support daily operations and logistics 4. Marketing & Communications Intern (X1) 📍 Johannesburg (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Support marketing and social media activities Create content Help grow online presence Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be 18 – 34 years old Be a South African citizen Have Matric (Grade 12 / N4) or higher qualification Have a passion for arts and youth development Be willing to learn and take initiative 👉 No previous formal employment required Required Documents Applicants must submit: Updated CV Certified copy of South African ID Certified copies of qualifications (or academic record if still studying) Proof of residence Reference letters Any relevant certificates or short courses Voluntary disclosure (if applicable) Proof of DSD Form 30 application/clearance (if required) Important Compliance Note Shortlisted candidates will undergo background and verification checks A DSD Form 30 clearance is required for roles involving child-facing work Only candidates with valid clearance will be appointed How to Apply 📧 Email your application to: info@assitej.org.za Important Notes Closing date: 29 April 2026 Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Ensure all required documents are submitted Why Apply for ASSITEJ Internships? Gain experience in the arts and development sector Build professional and administrative skills Work on meaningful projects impacting youth Grow your career in a creative and social environment

raw_data.description_full: Harmony Gold invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Learnership Programme 2026/2027. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 30 April 2026 Learnership Location: South Africa Learnership Overview Harmony Gold, one of the largest gold mining companies in South Africa, is offering learnership opportunities for candidates who want to build careers in artisan and engineering trades . This programme provides structured training and workplace exposure, helping learners gain the practical skills required in the mining industry. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Available Learnership Fields Applicants can apply in the following trades: Rigger Boilermaker / Plater Fitter Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Technical Grade 12 OR Grade 12 with Mathematics and Physical Science (minimum 50%) OR N2 qualification (with relevant trade theory subjects) OR NCV Level 4 with Mathematics (minimum 50%) and related trade theory Note: Maths Literacy is not accepted Additional Requirements Applicants must also: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Provide proof of residence Be medically fit Undergo psychometric assessments Have good English communication skills Be able to work in a team Be willing to work overtime and standby when required Follow health and safety protocols Added Advantages The following will strengthen your application: N6 or Diploma in Engineering Studies Driver’s licence and reliable transport Previous qualification in the chosen trade Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be contacted Applicants must undergo a medical fitness assessment Candidates will attend an interview If you do not receive feedback within 3 months after the closing date , consider your application unsuccessful. Required Documents Applicants may be required to submit: Certified copy of South African ID Certified copy of Matric certificate Certified copies of qualifications Updated CV Cover letter How to Apply Email your application to: DT.Motloung@harmony.co.za Important Notes Ensure all documents are certified and complete Submit your application before 30 April 2026 Incomplete applications may not be considered Why Choose Harmony Gold Learnerships? This learnership provides: Practical training in high-demand mining trades Industry-recognised experience A pathway into long-term employment in mining and engineering sectors

raw_data.description_full: Steinmüller Africa invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Apprenticeship Programme 2026 (Boilermaking or Welding). (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 22 May 2026 Apprenticeship Location: Bethal, Mpumalanga, South Africa About the Steinmüller Africa Apprenticeship Programme 2026 Steinmüller Africa, in partnership with Bilfinger Intervalve Africa, is offering apprenticeship opportunities aimed at developing skilled artisans within local communities near Kriel Power Station. This programme combines theoretical training and practical workplace experience to help learners qualify as artisans in Boilermaking or Welding. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Duration Minimum of 36 months (3 years) structured apprenticeship training Apprenticeship Overview Participants will: Complete merSETA accredited theoretical and practical modules Gain on-the-job training and workplace experience Prepare for and complete a trade test Qualify as a Certified Artisan (NQF Level 4) upon successful completion Key Responsibilities Complete all assigned training modules and workplace tasks Participate in practical learning and production support Work under supervision of mentors and training officers Assist with site and production-related duties when required Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following: Grade 12 with Mathematics (not Mathematical Literacy) and Physical Science or N2 qualification N2 with relevant subjects (Mathematics, Engineering Science, Platers Theory, Platers Drawings) is advantageous Must be 35 years or younger Physically and medically fit Clear criminal record Must reside within a 50 km radius of Kriel Power Station in Mpumalanga Must be willing to relocate to Bethal if not currently residing nearby Required Documents Applicants must submit certified copies of: Updated CV Grade 12 Certificate N2 Certificate and statement of results (if applicable) South African ID Proof of residence (municipal account or affidavit – mandatory) Important Notes This opportunity is only open to Employment Equity (EE) candidates No accommodation or transport will be provided Steinmüller Africa and Bilfinger Intervalve Africa will never ask for payment to process applications How to Apply Click here to apply for the Steinmüller Africa Apprenticeships 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Make sure you meet all requirements and submit all required documents before the closing date.

 The Department of Employment and Labour invites South African job seekers to apply for the Client Service Officer
raw_data.description_full: The Department of Employment and Labour invites South African job seekers to apply for the Client Service Officer: Assessor (X7 Posts) vacancy. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Closing Date: 12 May 2026 at 16:00 (walk-in) and 23:59 (online) Location: Kariega Labour Centre (Eastern Cape, South Africa) Overview of the Job Opportunity This is a great entry-level government job opportunity for candidates with Matric (Grade 12) and no prior experience required. The Department is recruiting 7 Client Service Officers (Assessors) who will support UIF (Unemployment Insurance Fund) services across multiple satellite offices. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Salary: R280 278 per annum Reference: POST 14/23 Type: Full-time Government Job Job Locations (X7 Posts) All posts are based at Kariega Labour Centre , but successful candidates will be stationed at the following satellite offices: Joubertina Louterwater Misgund Hankey Patensie Jeffreys Bay Humansdorp Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: Grade 12 / Senior Certificate No experience required Knowledge Required Candidates should have basic knowledge of: Unemployment Insurance Act UIF Contributions Act Public Financial Management Act (PFMA) POPIA (Protection of Personal Information Act) UIF policies and procedures Batho Pele Principles Skills and Competencies Applicants should demonstrate: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Good communication skills (verbal and written) Computer literacy Interpersonal skills Telephone etiquette Customer service skills Time management Conflict management Analytical thinking Ability to interpret UIF legislation Key Duties Successful candidates will be responsible for: Providing screening services to clients Processing UIF benefit applications Registering payment continuation forms Performing administrative duties Supporting UIF service delivery at Labour Centres Enquiries For more information, contact: Ms W Koba 📞 Tel: (041) 995 7047 How to Apply Submit your application to: By Post: Deputy Director: Labour Centre Operations P.O. Box 562 Kariega 6230 Hand Delivery: 15A Chase Street Kariega 6230 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Email Applications: 📧 Jobs-ECUHG@labour.gov.za Important Notes This is a re-advertisement , previous applicants are encouraged to re-apply Employment Equity targets apply Preference will be given to white male candidates as per EE plan Ensure all application documents are complete and correct Why You Should Apply No experience required Stable government job with benefits Good starting salary ( R280,278 per year ) Opportunity to build a career in public service Gain experience in UIF and labour services

raw_data.description_full: TotalEnergies South Africa invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Supply Chain Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: Not specified (apply as soon as possible) Learnership Locations: Nelspruit, Ladysmith, Island View (Durban), Alrode, Cape Town, South Africa Learnership Overview The TotalEnergies Supply Chain Learnership Programme is a 12-month workplace-based training programme designed to give young people practical experience in logistics, operations, and supply chain environments. This programme bridges the gap between classroom learning and real-world work experience within a global energy company , making it an excellent stepping stone into supply chain careers. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Details Organisation: TotalEnergies South Africa Duration: 12 Months Training Type: Workplace-based training Focus Area: Supply Chain & Operations Learnership Locations Opportunities are available at the following operational sites: Nelspruit Ladysmith Island View Terminal (Durban) Alrode (Gauteng) Cape Town Applicants are encouraged to apply for locations closest to where they live. What You Will Gain During the programme, learners will gain: Practical Work Experience Exposure to logistics coordination Stock movement and warehouse processes Operational documentation Safety compliance procedures Workplace Skills Development Teamwork and communication Time management and reliability Accuracy and attention to detail Professional work behaviour Career Opportunities After completion, you can pursue roles such as: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Logistics Assistant Warehouse Assistant Stock Controller Trainee Operations Clerk Supply Chain Administrator Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: Grade 12 (Matric) Basic computer literacy (MS Office) Good communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work in a team Willingness to learn and follow instructions Added Advantage Candidates with qualifications or studies in the following fields will have an advantage: Logistics Supply Chain Management Operations Management Transport Management Industrial Studies Ideal Candidate Profile This programme is suitable for: Recently matriculated youth Unemployed individuals seeking workplace experience Candidates interested in logistics and operations careers Individuals living near industrial or logistics hubs Required Documents Prepare the following documents: Certified copy of ID Certified Matric certificate Updated CV Academic transcripts (if applicable) Proof of residence (if required) Short motivation letter How to Apply APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN NELSPRUIT HERE (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN LADYSMITH HERE APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN ISLAND VIEW TERMINAL HERE APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN ISLAND VIEW TERMINAL HERE APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN ALRODE HERE APPLY FOR THE TOTALENERGIES SUPPLY CHAIN LEARNERSHIP IN CAPE TOWN HERE Important Notes Apply as early as possible as positions may fill quickly Ensure all documents are complete and certified Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Why Choose TotalEnergies Learnership? This learnership provides: Hands-on experience in a global company Exposure to real supply chain operations Strong foundation for long-term careers in logistics and operations

raw_data.description_full: If you have an S4 qualification in engineering , the Eskom Learner – Plant Maintenance Programme 2026 is a powerful opportunity to gain hands-on experience in South Africa’s energy sector. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This programme is ideal for candidates looking to build a career in power plant operations, maintenance, and technical engineering environments . Quick Overview Position: Learner – Plant Maintenance Company: Eskom Reference Number: eskom_004458 Number of Positions: X10 Location: Various sites across South Africa Contract Duration: 2 Years Stipend: Market-related Closing Date: 1 May 2026 About the Programme This is a 2-year structured learning programme under Eskom’s Turbo Gen Services division. Successful candidates will: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Receive practical on-site training Work with experienced technicians and engineers Develop real-world plant maintenance skills Minimum Requirements To qualify, you must have: S4 qualification in one of the following: Mechatronics Mechanical Engineering Electrical Engineering Electronics (Control & Instrumentation) 👉 Advantage: Electrical (Power Plant) specialisation Skills Required Eskom is looking for candidates with: Engineering and technical skills Ability to read technical drawings and specifications Knowledge of measuring equipment Computer literacy Basic planning skills Key Competencies Strong communication skills Attention to detail Ability to work under pressure Problem-solving mindset Teamwork and adaptability What You Will Learn During the programme, you will gain experience in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Plant maintenance systems Mechanical and electrical measurements Quality control processes Equipment and tool usage Engineering standards and compliance Personal Attributes Successful applicants should be: Honest and reliable Self-motivated Willing to learn Able to take initiative Why You Should Apply This Eskom programme offers: Valuable engineering work experience Exposure to the energy and power generation sector A strong foundation for future technical careers A 2-year structured training pathway How to Apply 👉 Apply here: 📧 For enquiries: recruitmenteri@eskom.co.za Important Notes No prior work experience is required Ensure all qualifications are completed (S4 level) Apply before the closing date to avoid missing out

raw_data.description_full: If you are interested in technology, cybersecurity, or IT , this learnership is one of the best entry-level opportunities available in 2026. The programme is offered by MANCOSA (PTY) LTD in partnership with The Transcendance Group (TTG) and is designed to help unemployed youth enter the fast-growing cybersecurity industry . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Quick Overview Programme: Cybersecurity Learnership Company: MANCOSA / The Transcendance Group Location: Cape Town, Western Cape Duration: 12 Months Contract Type: Fixed-Term Learnership Stipend: Paid (MICTSETA-aligned) Closing Date: 28 April 2026 About the Programme This is a structured, accredited learnership , not just a job. It combines: Formal classroom training Practical workplace experience Real-world cybersecurity exposure 👉 High-performing learners may be offered permanent job opportunities after completion , making this a strong career pathway. What You Will Learn During the programme, you will gain hands-on experience in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Cybersecurity Fundamentals Monitoring basic security systems Understanding threats and vulnerabilities Supporting IT security processes Practical IT Security Skills Conducting basic system checks Assisting with incident reporting Supporting compliance activities Workplace Experience Participating in real projects Working with IT teams Building a portfolio of evidence Minimum Requirements To qualify, you must: Have Grade 12 (Matric) Be unemployed Be between 18 – 35 years old Be a South African citizen Have basic computer skills Be willing to commit to the full programme Advantageous (But Not Required) IT-related qualification Basic knowledge of computers or networks Interest in cybersecurity or coding Previous digital skills training Skills & Competencies Needed Problem-solving ability Attention to detail Willingness to learn Basic technical thinking Communication and teamwork Programme Structure (Hybrid) The learnership uses a hybrid learning model , including: Classroom-based training Workplace-based experience Some online learning components Career Opportunities After Completion This programme can lead to entry-level roles such as: IT Support Technician Cybersecurity Assistant Security Operations Support Digital Services Freelancer 👉 Top performers may be placed directly into cybersecurity-related jobs . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Why You Should Apply High-demand tech career path No experience required Earn a monthly stipend Gain real workplace experience Opportunity for employment after completion How to Apply 👉 Apply here:

raw_data.description_full: If you have Matric and strong communication skills , the Bestmed Client Services Learnership Programme 2026 is a great entry-level opportunity to gain real workplace experience in healthcare administration and customer service . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This 12-month programme is part of the Youth Employment Service (YES) initiative and is designed to help unemployed youth build practical skills, work experience, and a strong CV . Quick Overview Programme: Bestmed Client Services Learnership (YES Initiative) Duration: 12 months Minimum Qualification: Grade 12 (Matric) Experience Required: None Sector: Healthcare administration / Call Centre / Customer Service Priority Candidates: Persons living with disabilities What You Will Learn This learnership combines training + real workplace experience , making it highly valuable for beginners. Customer Communication Skills You will learn how to: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Communicate professionally with clients Handle complaints and queries Provide accurate information Maintain service etiquette Call Centre Operations You will gain exposure to: Answering inbound calls Logging service requests Escalating queries Meeting response time targets Healthcare Administration Basics You will learn about: Medical scheme benefits Claims support processes Membership systems Compliance in healthcare communication Problem-Solving Skills You will develop: Issue resolution techniques Communication follow-ups Information verification skills Decision-making ability YES Programme Certification By completing this programme, you gain YES certification , which helps your CV stand out because it shows: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Workplace readiness Professional discipline Real work experience Employability skills Minimum Requirements To apply, you must: Have Grade 12 (Matric) Be unemployed Be willing to learn and grow Have good communication skills No experience is required , making this ideal for beginners. Who Should Apply? This programme is perfect for: School leavers First-time job seekers TVET graduates Youth interested in office-based work Anyone interested in healthcare or customer service careers Required Documents Prepare these before applying: Certified ID copy Certified Matric certificate Updated CV Proof of disability (if applicable) Proof of residence (if required) SARS tax number (if available) How to Apply 👉 Apply here: Why You Should Apply This learnership gives you: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Real workplace experience Exposure to the healthcare sector Valuable customer service skills A strong CV boost through YES certification Better chances of future employment

raw_data.description_full: TWK Agri invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Graduate Trainee Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Application Closing Date: Not specified (apply as soon as possible) Programme Location: South Africa Programme Overview TWK Agri is offering exciting graduate trainee opportunities for individuals who want to build a career in the agriculture, business, and financial services sectors . As a diversified organisation operating in agriculture, forestry, grain, and financial services, TWK Agri provides hands-on experience, structured training, and career development in a professional environment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Available Graduate Trainee Opportunities Graduate Trainee: Enterprise Development Degree in: Agriculture Agribusiness Economics Development Studies Rural Development Graduate Trainee: Insurance Degree in a relevant field Economics major is advantageous Graduate Trainee: Credit Degree in: Credit Finance Related fields Graduate Trainee: IT Degree in: Information Technology Computer Science Information Systems Graduate Trainee: Procurement Degree in: Supply Chain Management Business Management Graduate Trainee: Retail Degree in: Marketing Business Management Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be a South African graduate Hold a relevant Degree qualification Have strong communication and interpersonal skills Be willing to learn and grow within the organisation What You Will Gain Practical experience in the agricultural and business sectors Exposure to real workplace environments Skills development and career growth opportunities A foundation for long-term employment How to Apply NB. Use the links given below to Apply Graduate Trainee: Enterprise Development Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Enterprise Development Graduate Trainee: Insurance Pretoria: Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Insurance Nelspruit: Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Insurance Graduate Trainee: Credit Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Credit Graduate Trainee: IT Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: IT Graduate Trainee: Procurement Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Procurement Graduate Trainee: Retail Mpumalanga: Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Retail KwaZulu-Natal: Apply Online for the Graduate Trainee: Retail Important Notes Apply as early as possible as no closing date is specified Ensure your CV and qualifications match the position you are applying for Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Why Choose TWK Agri? TWK Agri is a well-established company that offers: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Diverse career opportunities Hands-on industry experience Growth within agriculture and business sectors

raw_data.description_full: Dr. Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma Local Municipality invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 06 May 2026 at 16:30 Internship Location: Dr. Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma Local Municipality, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa Internship Overview The NDZ Municipality Internship Programme 2025/2026 is a 12-month structured programme designed to provide graduates with practical experience in municipal finance, environmental management, and community services . Successful candidates will receive a monthly stipend of R5 000 , while the Municipal Finance Management Intern receives R75 000 per annum . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Available Internship Opportunities 1. Municipal Finance Management Intern (X1) Department: Finance Salary: R75 000 per annum Requirements: Grade 12 National Diploma or Degree in Accounting or equivalent Communication skills (English & IsiZulu) Computer literacy 2. Data Cleanser Intern (X2) Department: Finance Requirements: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 Diploma or Degree in Financial Management, Accounting or related field 3. Revenue Cashier Intern (X1) Department: Finance Requirements: Grade 12 Financial-related qualification 4. Payroll / Expenditure Intern (X1) Department: Finance Requirements: Grade 12 Financial-related qualification 5. Environmental Management Intern (X1) Department: Public Works & Basic Services Requirements: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 Diploma or Degree in Environmental Management or related field 6. Pound Intern (X1) Department: Community & Social Services Requirements: Grade 12 Diploma or Degree in Agriculture or related field Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be South African citizens Reside within the municipality’s jurisdiction Hold a relevant qualification for the position applied for Be unemployed Have good communication and computer skills Key Learning Areas Interns will gain exposure to: Budgeting and financial reporting Revenue and expenditure management Asset management Procurement and supply chain processes Environmental and municipal service delivery systems Required Documents Applicants must submit: Completed municipal application form (from www.ndz.gov.za ) Updated CV with 3 references Certified copies of qualifications Certified copy of ID (not older than 6 months) How to Apply Click here to download the application form: www.ndz.gov.za 📮 Post your application to: Mr. S.J Sondezi Acting Municipal Manager Dr. Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma Local Municipality P.O. Box 62 Creighton, 3263 📍 Or hand deliver to: Municipality Building, Main Street, Creighton Arbuckle Street, Himeville Important Notes No email or fax applications accepted Applications must be submitted before 06 May 2026 at 16:30 Late or incomplete applications will be disqualified If no response is received, consider your application unsuccessful Why Apply for NDZ Municipality Internship? Gain experience in local government operations Build skills in finance and service delivery Improve employability in the public sector Earn a monthly stipend

raw_data.description_full: FlySafair invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Graduate Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: Not specified (Apply as soon as possible) Internship Location: Johannesburg, South Africa About the FlySafair Graduate Internship Programme The FlySafair Graduate Internship Programme 2026 offers graduates a chance to gain valuable workplace experience in the aviation industry . This 12-month programme is designed to equip young professionals with practical skills, industry exposure, and career development opportunities. FlySafair is one of South Africa’s leading low-cost airlines, making this a high-impact opportunity for graduates looking to enter a fast-paced and dynamic work environment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Available Internship Opportunities The programme offers placements in the following fields: Talent Acquisition Intern Supply Chain Intern Digital Solutions Engineer Intern Marketing Intern Logistics Intern Compliance Intern Legal Intern These roles provide exposure to different departments, allowing graduates to build experience aligned with their qualifications. Internship Duration Contract: 12 Months fixed-term Type: Graduate Internship Programme Key Responsibilities Interns will be expected to: Assist with daily operations and administrative tasks Support ongoing projects and team activities Conduct research when required Maintain task logs and reports Ensure compliance with company policies Collaborate with team members across departments Perform ad-hoc duties as assigned Minimum Requirements To qualify for the programme, applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 (Matric) A relevant Diploma or Degree Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Strong organisational and analytical skills Skills and Competencies Successful candidates should demonstrate: Professionalism and commitment Strong attention to detail Willingness to learn Customer service orientation Ability to work under pressure Good communication skills (verbal and written) Time management and reliability Why You Should Apply Gain real-world experience in the aviation sector Build practical skills across multiple business functions Enhance your CV with a recognised internship Work in a fast-paced and professional environment Improve your chances of future employment How to Apply Click here to apply for FlySafair Graduate Internships 2026 Important Notes Applications are open until positions are filled Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Ensure your CV is updated and relevant to the position applied for

raw_data.description_full: St Peter’s Prep School invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Teacher Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: Not specified Internship Location: Johannesburg, South Africa About the St Peter’s Prep Teacher Internship Programme The St Peter’s Prep Teacher Internship Programme is a unique long-term opportunity designed for aspiring educators who are passionate about teaching young children. This programme combines: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Full-time academic study (B.Ed degree – 4 years) Daily classroom-based practical experience Mentorship from experienced teachers The goal is to develop professional, skilled, and inspirational teachers who can contribute meaningfully to South Africa’s education system. Programme Structure Start Date: January 2027 Duration: 4 Years Qualification: Bachelor of Education (B.Ed) Training Type: Practical + Academic learning Interns will gain hands-on experience in: Classroom teaching Lesson planning Learner engagement Educational development Key Responsibilities The programme aims to develop teachers who will: Apply modern teaching methods effectively Handle the emotional and social demands of teaching Work collaboratively in school environments Demonstrate professionalism and accountability Develop a strong growth mindset Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 / National Senior Certificate with degree admission Minimum APS score of 28 (excluding Life Orientation) English: 60% (Home Language) OR 70% (First Additional Language) Mathematics: 40% (Mathematics) OR 60% (Maths Literacy) Be a South African citizen Preferably be under the age of 27 Demonstrate financial need Ideal Candidate Profile You are a great fit if you: Have experience working with children Are motivated and proactive Enjoy working in a dynamic environment Have strong organisational and time management skills Are passionate about education Required Documents Applicants must submit: Matric Certificate (with university exemption) Cover letter explaining your interest in teaching Updated CV Certified copy of ID Important: Incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Click here to apply for St Peter’s Prep School Teacher Internship Programme 2026 Why You Should Apply Earn a fully recognised B.Ed degree Gain hands-on classroom experience Learn from experienced educators Build a long-term teaching career Contribute to education and nation-building

raw_data.description_full: The Building Company invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the W&RSETA Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: Not specified Learnership Location: South Africa About The Building Company W&RSETA Learnerships 2026 The Building Company is offering W&RSETA Learnership opportunities for unemployed South African youth who are interested in gaining workplace experience in the retail sector. Successful learners will attend theoretical training sessions and complete workplace duties related to retail operations. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Responsibilities Successful applicants will be expected to: Attend all theoretical training sessions facilitated by the training provider. Complete assessments, assignments, and Portfolio of Evidence (PoE) in line with W&RSETA requirements. Perform workplace duties related to retail operations, including assisting customers, merchandising and replenishing stock, supporting point-of-sale operations where applicable, and maintaining store cleanliness. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Follow company policies, procedures, and standard operating practices. Comply with health and safety requirements in the workplace. Maintain accurate logbook entries and track workplace experience. Demonstrate professional behaviour, punctuality, and consistent attendance. Engage with supervisors, mentors, and facilitators to support learning progress. Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); South African citizen, as per funding requirements. Unemployed and eligible for a W&RSETA learnership. Minimum qualification: Grade 12. Basic numeracy and literacy skills. Clear criminal record. Willingness to work retail hours, including weekends and public holidays where applicable. Must not have completed an unemployed learnership before. How to apply NB. Use the links given below to Apply Location: Kempton Park, Gauteng Apply Online for the The Building Company: W&RSETA Learnership Programme Location: Seapoint, Western Cape Apply Online for the The Building Company: W&RSETA Learnership Programme Location: Athlone Apply Online for the The Building Company: W&RSETA Learnership Programme Location: Knysna Apply Online for the The Building Company: W&RSETA Learnership Programme Location: Jane Furse Apply Online for the The Building Company: W&RSETA Learnership Programme Applications must be submitted online through The Building Company recruitment platform.

raw_data.description_full: Momentum Health invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Contact Centre Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 23 April 2026 Learnership Location: Bellville, Western Cape, South Africa Learnership Overview Momentum Health is offering a 12-month learnership programme designed to provide both theoretical training and practical workplace experience in the healthcare and insurance industry. Upon successful completion, learners will receive an Occupational Certificate: Health Care Benefits Advisor (NQF Level 5, SAQA ID 105030) . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Duration Start Date: 01 June 2026 End Date: 31 May 2027 Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following: Grade 12 (Matric) Mathematics (minimum 40%) or Mathematical Literacy (minimum 60%) English and another South African language (minimum 50%) Must be a South African citizen Must be between 18 and 30 years old Must be unemployed Must not be studying during the programme Must not have completed the same learnership before Must live within 30km of Bellville (Parc Du Cap Office) Must be able to commute independently Added Advantage Computer literacy Duties and Responsibilities Learning & Development Attend classroom and workplace training sessions Complete assessments and compile a portfolio of evidence Maintain logbooks of workplace experience Learn company systems, products, and procedures Client Service Assist clients with queries and provide accurate information Maintain high service standards Build relationships with clients and stakeholders Improve turnaround times and service delivery Teamwork & Growth Collaborate with colleagues Participate in workplace initiatives Support innovation and improvement Take ownership of personal development Financial Awareness Support cost-effective processes Identify risks and escalate issues Contribute to operational efficiency Skills and Competencies Strong communication skills Attention to detail Problem-solving ability Ability to work under pressure Customer-focused mindset Teamwork and collaboration Required Documents Applicants must upload: Certified copy of ID (not older than 3 months) Certified copy of Matric certificate Doctor’s letter (if applying as a person with disability) Why Apply? This learnership offers: Recognised NQF Level 5 qualification Valuable experience in healthcare and insurance Practical customer service skills Improved career opportunities in medical aid administration How to Apply Click here to apply for Momentum Health Contact Centre Learnership 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Important Notes Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Completion of the programme does not guarantee permanent employment People living with disabilities are encouraged to apply

raw_data.location: Siza Water Service Area, South Africa
raw_data.description_full: Siza Water invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Plumber Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 23 April 2026 Learnership Location: Siza Water Service Area, South Africa Learnership Overview Siza Water (A SAWW Company) is offering 2 Plumber Learnership opportunities for unemployed youth who are interested in building a career in plumbing and infrastructure maintenance. This programme provides hands-on technical training within a real water services environment, preparing learners for future opportunities in artisan trades and municipal infrastructure roles. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Details Position: Plumber Learnership (X2 Posts) Reference Number: NET/02/2026 Organisation: Siza Water Duration: Structured learnership programme Focus Area: Plumbing and water infrastructure maintenance Key Responsibilities Successful learners will assist the Network Department with: Water pipeline maintenance Infrastructure repair support Field inspections and fault identification Plumbing installation tasks Operational maintenance duties Promoting safe worksite practices Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: Matric (Grade 12) Valid Code 8 Driver’s Licence Added Advantage: Plumbing experience Construction or infrastructure exposure Basic knowledge of maintenance tools and safety Preference Criteria Preference will be given to: Applicants residing within the Siza Water service area This is important as local candidates can respond quickly to field operations and support community-based service delivery. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Required Documents Applicants should prepare the following: Certified copy of ID Certified Matric certificate Copy of driver’s licence Proof of residence Short CV (maximum 2 pages) How to Apply Click here to apply for Siza Water Plumber Learnership Programme 2026: https://www.sizawater.com/vacancies OR Hand deliver your application to: Human Resource Department Siza Water Important Notes: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Your CV must not exceed 2 pages Ensure all documents are correctly certified Submit your application before 23 April 2026 Why This Learnership Matters This learnership is more than just training—it is a career pathway into high-demand artisan skills such as plumbing and infrastructure maintenance. These skills are essential across municipalities, water utilities, and construction industries in South Africa.

raw_data.description_full: If you are an unemployed young person in South Africa looking to gain practical skills and workplace experience, now is the time to apply. Two excellent learnership opportunities are currently open, with deadlines fast approaching before 23 April 2026 . These programmes offer valuable training, industry exposure, and a pathway into long-term employment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Below are the top 2 must-apply learnerships you should not miss. 1. Lephalale TVET College IT End-User Computing Learnerships 2026 Lephalale TVET College invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the IT End-User Computing Learnership Programme 2026. Learnership Application Closing Date: 21 April 2026 at 16:00 Learnership Location: Lephalale Campus, Limpopo, South Africa (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Overview This learnership is ideal for individuals interested in building a career in Information Technology. It focuses on End-User Computing , equipping learners with practical digital and computer skills needed in modern workplaces. Key Benefits Gain hands-on IT skills Improve computer literacy and workplace readiness Increase chances of employment in the tech industry Minimum Requirements Must be unemployed Basic understanding of computers South African citizen How to Apply Click here to apply for Lephalale TVET College IT End-User Computing Learnerships 2026 2. Sibanye-Stillwater Miner Learnerships 2026 Sibanye-Stillwater invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Miner Learnership Programme 2026. Learnership Application Closing Date: 23 April 2026 Learnership Location: South Africa (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Overview This learnership is perfect for those interested in the mining sector , offering structured training and real-world experience in mining operations. Key Benefits Exposure to the mining industry Practical on-site training Opportunity to build a long-term career in mining Minimum Requirements Must be unemployed Willingness to work in mining environments South African citizen How to Apply Click here to apply for Sibanye Stillwater Miner Learnerships 2026: Final Thoughts These learnerships are time-sensitive opportunities that can help you kickstart your career. Whether you are interested in IT or mining, both programmes provide valuable skills and experience. Make sure to apply before the closing dates to avoid missing out.

raw_data.description_full: The Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA) invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Wealth Management Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 25 April 2026 Learnership Location: Pretoria, Gauteng, South Africa Programme Overview The FSCA Wealth Management Learnership Programme is a 12-month structured programme designed to equip matriculants with practical skills and theoretical knowledge in the financial services sector. This programme focuses on developing future professionals in wealth management and financial advisory services , while supporting the FSCA’s mission to promote fair financial practices and protect consumers. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); What the Programme Offers Successful learners will: Receive formal training in wealth management Gain workplace experience in FSCA business units Work under the guidance of experienced mentors Participate in structured, competency-based learning Develop skills for entry-level roles in financial services Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen (valid ID required) Must have completed Grade 12 (Matric) Must be currently unemployed Must not have participated in a learnership before Must have a strong interest in the financial services sector Added Advantage Basic computer literacy Good communication skills Employment Equity The FSCA is committed to transformation and encourages applications from previously disadvantaged groups and persons living with disabilities . Duration and Stipend Duration: 9 – 12 months (fixed-term contract) Stipend: Market-related allowance Why Apply? This learnership offers: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Valuable workplace experience in finance Industry-relevant skills in wealth management Mentorship and professional development Improved career opportunities in the financial sector How to Apply Click here to apply for FSCA Wealth Management Learnership Programme 2026 Important Notes Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted FSCA reserves the right not to make an appointment Ensure all required documents (ID, Matric certificate, CV) are submitted
