raw_data.company: Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa (CCBSA) Visual Merchandiser
raw_data.description_full: Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Visual Merchandiser Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 21 April 2026 Learnership Location: Upington, Northern Cape, South Africa Learnership Overview This 12-month learnership programme is designed to provide structured workplace learning in sales and marketing, with a strong focus on visual merchandising and customer service . Learners will gain practical skills in retail execution, product display, and customer engagement while working closely with sales teams. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Key Responsibilities In-Store Execution & Merchandising Execute daily store visits according to planned schedules Implement merchandising standards and product displays Ensure product availability, visibility, and quality Conduct stock rotation and maintain brand standards Complete iRED surveys and meet execution targets Customer Support Assist customers with ordering via the MyCCBA platform Support promotional activities and campaigns Engage with customers to understand preferences and behaviours Team Collaboration Work with Sales Team Members on daily activities Receive guidance and feedback from Account Developers Provide support where needed as directed by management Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following: Grade 12 (Matric) with: Mathematics or Maths Literacy (minimum 40%) English and a second language (minimum 40%) Must be a South African citizen Must be unemployed at the time of appointment Must be aged between 18 and 35 years Must not be studying or part of another learnership Must not have completed the same qualification before Must have no criminal or adverse credit record Must be available to work six days a week Added Advantage Driver’s license Post-matric qualification in Sales & Marketing Skills and Competencies Communication and interpersonal skills Customer service orientation Attention to detail Ability to work under pressure Teamwork and collaboration Programme Details Duration: 12 months Qualification: Minimum of 120 credits required Industry: Sales and Marketing / Retail Required Documents Applicants must submit: Detailed CV Certified copy of Matric certificate Certified copies of qualifications (if applicable) Copy of Driver’s License (if available) Why Apply? This learnership offers: Practical retail and sales experience Skills in merchandising and customer engagement Exposure to a leading global beverage company Improved chances of employment in sales and marketing How to Apply Click here to apply for Coca-Cola Visual Merchandiser Learnership 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Important Notes Only complete applications with all required documents will be considered Management may use additional criteria for shortlisting Employment Equity candidates will be given preference

raw_data.description_full: Eskom invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Learner – Plant Maintenance Programme 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 1 May 2026 Internship Location: Various Sites, South Africa About the Eskom Plant Maintenance Programme The Eskom Learner Programme is a 2-year structured technical training opportunity within the Turbo Gen Services (Plant Maintenance) division. This programme is designed to provide hands-on experience in engineering, maintenance, and power plant operations , preparing learners for careers in the energy sector. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Overview Position: Learner – Plant Maintenance Reference Number: eskom_004458 Number of Posts: 10 Duration: 2 Years Department: Plant Maintenance Business Unit: Turbo Gen Services Remuneration: Market-related stipend Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: S4 Qualification in one of the following: Mechatronics Mechanical Engineering Electrical Engineering Electronics (C&I) Advantage: Electrical (Power Plant) specialisation Experience: Not required Required Skills and Knowledge Technical Knowledge: Ability to use mechanical, electrical, and instrumentation measuring tools Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and tolerances Basic understanding of quality systems Knowledge of technical specifications and standards Skills: Strong engineering and technical skills Good communication and interpersonal skills Ability to use basic hand tools Basic planning and computer skills Personal Attributes: Ability to work under pressure Honest and reliable Adaptable and proactive Strong work ethic and initiative Key Learning Areas Participants will gain experience in: Plant maintenance and engineering operations Reading and interpreting technical drawings Equipment measurement and diagnostics Working with international engineering standards Maintaining high-quality workmanship How to Apply Click here to apply for Eskom Learner – Plant Maintenance Programme 2026 For Enquiries: 📧 recruitmenteri@eskom.co.za (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Why You Should Apply Joining Eskom offers: Hands-on technical training in the energy sector Exposure to power plant maintenance operations A strong foundation for engineering careers Opportunity to work with experienced professionals

raw_data.description_full: O.R. Tambo District Municipality invites South African unemployed graduates residing within the municipal jurisdiction to apply for the Graduate Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 01 May 2026 Internship Location: O.R. Tambo District Municipality, Eastern Cape, South Africa Internship Overview The O.R. Tambo District Municipality Graduate Internship Programme is a 24-month structured programme designed to provide practical workplace experience to unemployed graduates across various municipal departments. Successful candidates will receive a monthly stipend of R6,000 while gaining valuable experience in public service and local government operations. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Details Programme Duration: 24 Months Stipend: R6,000 per month Eligibility: Must reside within O.R. Tambo District Municipality Covered Local Municipalities: King Sabata Dalindyebo Mhlontlo Nyandeni Port St Johns Ngquza Hill Available Internship Fields Internship opportunities are available across multiple departments, including: Infrastructure, Water & Sanitation Office Management & Technology / Social Science Civil Engineering Public Management Strategic Planning & Governance Public Administration / Management Law (LLB) Social Sciences Community Services Environmental Science Public Administration Social Sciences Environmental Health Corporate Services Human Resources Labour Relations Psychology / Social Work Logistics / Transport ICT / Records Management Regional Economic Development & Planning (REDP) Tourism Economics / Social Sciences Business Management / Administration Town / Regional Planning Budget and Treasury Office Finance Accounting Supply Chain Management Economics / Logistics Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be South African citizens Reside within the O.R. Tambo District Municipality jurisdiction Have a relevant Diploma or Degree qualification Provide proof of residence from a ward councillor Required Documents Applicants must submit: Updated CV Certified copy of ID Certified copies of qualifications Proof of residence (from ward councillor) Cover letter indicating the position applied for Selection Process Shortlisted candidates may be required to: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Undergo qualification verification Complete credit and security checks Participate in assessments Sign employment agreements How to Apply Click here to apply for O.R. Tambo District Municipality Internship Programme 2026 Or visit: https://www.ortambodm.gov.za Important Notes Applications must be submitted before 23:59 on 01 May 2026 Late or incomplete applications will not be considered If no feedback is received within 90 days , consider your application unsuccessful Canvassing councillors or officials will result in disqualification Why Apply for This Internship? This programme offers: Valuable experience in local government and public service Exposure to multiple departments and career paths A stepping stone into permanent municipal employment

raw_data.description_full: Mezzanine invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 31 March 2026 Bursary Location: Western Cape, South Africa About the Mezzanine Bursary Programme 2026 The Mezzanine Bursary Programme is an academic merit bursary aimed at supporting talented students who want to study Computer Science but may lack the financial resources to complete their studies. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This bursary is specifically designed for first-year students studying full-time at recognised universities in the Western Cape. In addition to financial support, successful candidates will benefit from job shadowing opportunities and networking with professionals at Mezzanine, helping them build industry exposure early in their careers. What the Bursary Covers The bursary may cover: Tuition fees University accommodation Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Must be a South African citizen by birth Must be residing in the Western Cape Must be unemployed Must have passed Grade 12 with a minimum average of 65% Must be enrolled as a first-year student for full-time undergraduate studies Must be studying towards a Computer Science qualification Must be accepted at a recognised and accredited university in the Western Cape Must not be receiving any other bursary for the same academic year Must have no criminal record Must have a clear credit record Required Documents Applicants must upload the following documents: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Certified copy of South African ID Certified copy of Matric certificate Motivational letter Proof of registration or acceptance for the year of study Latest fee statement or quotation Proof of household income (latest 3 months payslips or affidavit if unemployed) Selection Criteria Bursaries will be awarded based on: Academic merit Financial need Mezzanine’s employment equity plan Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If you do not receive feedback within 30 days after the closing date , consider your application unsuccessful. How to Apply Click here to apply for Mezzanine Bursary Programme 2026:

raw_data.description_full: The South African National Biodiversity Institute invites South African unemployed individuals to apply for the Greening Programme 2026 under the Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP). (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Closing Date: 30 April 2026 (16:00) Programme Location: Various National Botanical Gardens across South Africa Programme Overview The SANBI Greening Programme 2026 provides short-term employment opportunities for both skilled and unskilled participants . This programme focuses on: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Environmental maintenance Biodiversity conservation Garden development Community upliftment It is part of the EPWP initiative , aimed at creating income opportunities while improving environmental sustainability. Available Positions Unskilled Participants Stipend: R150 per day Contract Duration: Until 31 March 2027 Skilled Participants Stipend: R200 per day Contract Duration: Until 31 March 2027 👉 Skilled applicants must have experience in areas such as: Chainsaw or brush cutter operation Herbicide application Painting, paving, bricklaying General maintenance or construction work Locations & Posts Opportunities are available across multiple provinces: Gauteng Pretoria National Botanical Garden Walter Sisulu National Botanical Garden (Roodepoort) National Zoological Garden (Pretoria) Limpopo Thohoyandou National Botanical Garden Mphaphuli Cycad Nature Reserve Mokopane BCC KwaZulu-Natal Pietermaritzburg National Botanical Garden Mpumalanga Lowveld National Botanical Garden (Mbombela) Free State Free State National Botanical Garden (Bloemfontein) Western Cape Kirstenbosch, Harold Porter & Karoo Desert Botanical Gardens Northern Cape Hantam National Botanical Garden Eastern Cape Kwelera National Botanical Garden Minimum Requirements Applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be South African citizens Reside within the local municipality of the garden Provide proof of residence Be physically fit for outdoor/manual work Be willing to work in a team and independently Be available for interviews if shortlisted Duties and Responsibilities Participants will: Maintain gardens and landscapes Remove invasive plant species Clean and maintain pathways Assist with tree planting and conservation activities Support environmental protection initiatives Required Documents Applicants must submit: Completed SANBI application form Updated CV (maximum 2 pages) Proof of residence How to Apply Click here to download SANBI application form 📧 Email applications to: epwp@sanbi.org.za 📍 Or submit by hand/post to the relevant botanical garden office (based on your reference number) Important Notes Use the correct reference number for your location Submit your application before 30 April 2026 at 16:00 Preference will be given to: Women Youth Persons with disabilities If no feedback is received within 30 days , consider your application unsuccessful Why Apply for SANBI Greening Programme? Earn daily income while gaining experience Work in environmental and conservation projects Build skills in landscaping and maintenance Contribute to protecting South Africa’s biodiversity

raw_data.description_full: Sekhukhune Development Agency (SDA) invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Photovoltaic (PV) Solar Installation Green Card Certification Training Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Closing Date: Not specified (Apply as soon as possible) Training Location: Sekhukhune Skills Centre, Limpopo, South Africa About the PV Solar Installation Training Programme The SDA PV Solar Installation Training Programme is a high-impact technical training opportunity designed to prepare candidates for careers in South Africa’s rapidly growing renewable energy sector. With increasing demand for solar installations due to load shedding and energy transitions, this programme equips learners with: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Practical solar installation skills Industry-recognised certification Exposure to real-world renewable energy systems Successful participants will receive a PV Green Card Certification , which is widely recognised in the solar industry. Quick Overview Programme: PV Solar Installation Green Card Training Number of Posts: 40 Sector: Renewable Energy / Electrical Engineering Certification: PV Green Card (Industry-recognised) Training Venue: Sekhukhune Skills Centre Assessment Body: WCC Assessment Industry Exposure: Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) What is PV Green Card Certification? The PV Green Card is a recognised certification that proves competence in solar installation. It covers: Solar system safety Installation standards Wiring and system design Compliance and inspection procedures Mounting and installation techniques This certification significantly improves your employability in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Residential solar installations Municipal energy projects Commercial solar systems Renewable energy companies Training Content Participants will receive both theoretical and practical training in: Solar panel installation PV system wiring Safety compliance procedures System troubleshooting Installation sequencing Inspection and testing Hands-On Training Benefits This programme includes practical training , not just classroom learning. Learners will gain: Real equipment experience Supervised installation practice Technical mentorship Workplace-ready skills CSIR Exposure Participants will also benefit from industry exposure at the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) , including: Study tours Interaction with experts Solar technology demonstrations Exposure to advanced PV systems Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: N3 – N6 in Electrical Engineering OR Diploma in Electrical Engineering OR Degree in Electrical Engineering Advantage: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Trade Test in Electrical Engineering Required Documents Prepare the following: Certified copy of ID Updated CV Certified qualifications Trade Test certificate (if available) Proof of residence (if required) How to Apply Click here to apply for Sekhukhune Development Agency (SDA) PV Solar Installation Training Programme 2026: 👉 Alternative Application Method: Hand deliver your CV and qualifications at: Bareki Mall, Groblersdal Enquiries: 076 360 2091 Why You Should Apply Gain in-demand solar installation skills Earn a recognised PV Green Card certification Get hands-on training and industry exposure Enter the fast-growing renewable energy sector Improve your chances of employment or self-employment

raw_data.description_full: Hitachi Energy invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Electrical Apprenticeship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 30 April 2026 Apprenticeship Location: South Africa Apprenticeship Overview The Hitachi Energy Electrical Apprenticeship Programme 2026 is a career-launching opportunity for individuals who want to become qualified electricians in the energy and engineering sector. This programme combines practical on-site training, technical learning, and mentorship , helping you build a strong foundation in electrical engineering. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Details Company: Hitachi Energy Programme Type: Electrical Apprenticeship Duration: Structured multi-year apprenticeship Stipend: Monthly stipend provided What You Will Learn Electrical Testing & Fault Finding Identify and diagnose electrical faults Use diagnostic tools and equipment Perform system testing Tools & Equipment Usage Safe handling of electrical tools Use of measuring instruments Maintenance of equipment Workplace Safety & Compliance Occupational health and safety standards Environmental compliance Risk management practices Key Responsibilities Apprentices will: Assist qualified electricians Support installation and maintenance projects Conduct inspections Perform basic electrical repairs Work in teams with engineers and technicians Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: Grade 12 (Matric) Mathematics Physical Science Advantageous Qualifications N2 or N3 Electrical qualification Technical training background Skills and Competencies Strong interest in electrical work Problem-solving ability Good communication skills Ability to follow instructions Teamwork and adaptability How to Apply Click here to apply for Hitachi Energy Electrical Apprenticeship Programme 2026 Important Notes Apply before 30 April 2026 Ensure you meet all academic requirements Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Why Choose Hitachi Energy? Gain experience in a global energy company Learn high-demand electrical skills Receive structured technical training Build a long-term career in engineering

raw_data.company: South African Institute of Intellectual Property Law (SAIIPL)
raw_data.description_full: South African Institute of Intellectual Property Law (SAIIPL) invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 31 March 2026 Bursary Location: South Africa About the Bursary Provider – SAIIPL The South African Institute of Intellectual Property Law (SAIIPL) is a professional body representing practitioners in the field of Intellectual Property (IP) law. The organisation promotes high standards of expertise and ethical practice in areas such as trade marks, copyright, and patent law , while also contributing to the development of South Africa’s IP legal framework. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); About the SAIIPL Bursary Programme 2026 SAIIPL is inviting students to apply for its bursary programme for the 2026 academic year. A total of 4 bursaries will be awarded to students studying LLB or LLM degrees , with a strong interest in Intellectual Property Law. Bursaries will be allocated as follows: 2 bursaries: Patent Law 2 bursaries: Trade Mark and/or Copyright Law Bursary Value Each bursary is valued at R70 000 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The funding may be used towards outstanding tuition fees for the academic year. Programme Objectives This bursary programme aims to: Support previously disadvantaged students Develop future legal professionals in IP law Promote careers in trade mark, copyright, and patent law Strengthen South Africa’s intellectual property sector Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen or permanent resident Must be between 18 and 30 years old Must be studying towards an LLB or LLM degree Must be registered at a recognised South African university Must have strong academic potential Must be eligible for admission as an attorney in South Africa (in terms of the Legal Practice Act) Must have completed at least one WIPO course (e.g. DL001) and provide proof Must have a strong interest in Intellectual Property Law, including: Trade Marks Copyright Patents Designs Indigenous Knowledge Plant Breeders’ Rights Geographical Indicators Trade Secrets Must demonstrate financial need Required Supporting Documents Applicants must submit the following documents: Certified copy of ID or passport Certified academic transcripts for all years of study Proof of registration for the current or upcoming academic year Proof of residence Proof of financial need (household income) Curriculum Vitae (CV) Motivational letter (maximum 500 words) including: Interest in IP Law Whether applying for Patent or Trade Mark bursary Career aspirations in IP law Consent to an open and honest interview and publication thereof Letter of recommendation (optional) For Patent bursary applicants: proof of qualification to enrol for Patent Examinations How to Apply Click here to apply for SAIIPL Bursary South Africa 2026: saiipl@saiipl.co.za Email address: saiipl@saiipl.co.za Subject line: SAIIPL Bursary 2026 Attention: Ms Michelle Macguire (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); There is no application form . Applicants must submit all required documents via email. Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by 31 July 2026 . If you do not receive feedback by this date, consider your application unsuccessful. Closing Date The closing date for applications is 31 March 2026 . Late applications will not be accepted.

raw_data.description_full: South African Nursery Association (SANA) invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 31 March 2026 Bursary Location: South Africa About the Bursary Provider – SANA The South African Nursery Association (SANA) is a non-profit organisation established in 1947. It represents the interests of the horticulture and nursery industry in South Africa and works closely with related associations such as ABSTA, GCA, and BPGA. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); SANA promotes professionalism in the horticultural sector and maintains strong relationships with government departments on environmental and industry-related matters. About the SANA Bursary Programme 2026 SANA is inviting students to apply for its bursary programme for the 2026 academic year. The bursary is aimed at students pursuing studies in: Horticulture Landscaping This programme supports students who are passionate about the green industry and want to build careers in horticulture and environmental fields. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); What the Bursary Covers The bursary will cover: Tuition fees only Funding is paid directly to the institution. The bursary does not cover: Outstanding fees Accommodation Books Travel or allowances Registration or practical training costs Additional Benefits Successful bursary recipients will also receive free SANA membership , which includes: Industry newsletters and updates Digital SANA Magazine Invitations to SANA Trade Fairs in Gauteng and KwaZulu-Natal Networking opportunities within the nursery industry Membership is valid for 1 year and must be renewed annually. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Must have completed Matric Must be studying towards a Horticulture or Landscaping qualification Must preferably be studying at undergraduate level Must have completed all modules of the first academic year Must have achieved a minimum average of 60% per subject Must be registered for 2026 studies at a recognised South African institution Must be a SANA student member or apply for membership (free) Required Supporting Documents Applicants must submit the following: Certified copy of ID document Recent photograph (selfie or student card photo) Current academic record Certificates of previous studies Tertiary institution banking details (on official letterhead) Proof of registration for 2026 Completed SANA Student Membership Application Form Completed SANA Bursary Checklist Motivational letter (minimum 150 words) How to Apply Click here to apply for South African Nursery Association (SANA) Bursary South Africa 2026: ayanda@sana.co.za Email address: ayanda@sana.co.za Subject line: SANA Bursary 2026 Applicants must download and complete the SANA Bursary Application Form and submit it together with all required documents via email. Completed SANA Student Membership Application Form 2025 (.pdf) Completed SANA Bursary Checklist 2026 (.pdf) Selection Process Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews with SANA representatives in April 2026. Closing Date The closing date for applications is 31 March 2026 . Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted.

raw_data.description_full: If you’re a South African student looking for funding opportunities, now is the time to apply. Several bursaries for the 2026 and 2027 academic years are closing at the end of March 2026. Missing these deadlines could mean waiting another year. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Below is a list of top bursaries currently open, including key details and how to apply. Mbuyelo Coal Ntimeni Colliery Bursaries 2027 Mbuyelo Coal Ntimeni Colliery invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2027. Bursary Application Closing Date: 30 March 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This bursary is aimed at students pursuing studies related to mining and associated fields. It provides financial support to deserving students, helping them build careers in the mining industry. How to Apply Click here to apply for Mbuyelo Coal Ntimeni Colliery Bursaries 2027: Compensation Fund Bursaries 2026 The Compensation Fund, under the Department of Employment and Labour, invites South African students to apply for the Compensation Fund Bursary Programme 2026. Bursary Application Closing Date: 31 March 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This bursary supports students in various fields, particularly those aligned with labour, health, and occupational safety sectors. raw_data.description_full: Fine Music Radio invites South African students to apply for the FMR Bursary Trust Awards 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 12 June 2026 Location: South Africa (National) About the Bursary The Fine Music Radio (FMR) Bursary Trust Awards aim to support talented students pursuing studies in music performance. The bursary is awarded to students studying towards a Diploma or Degree in Music , with a focus on performance disciplines. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Fields Covered The bursary competition awards students in the following categories: African Music (Vocal/Instrumental) Classical Vocal Classical Instrumental Jazz Vocal/Instrumental Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be South African citizens Reside in South Africa Be studying towards a Diploma or Degree in Music Be registered at a recognised tertiary institution Bursary Competition Process First Round: Submit a pre-recorded audition video (YouTube – unlisted) Final Round: Selected candidates perform at Baxter Concert Hall (Cape Town) Performances will be recorded and broadcast on Fine Music Radio Applicants will be judged on performance, presentation, and musical ability Required Documents Applicants must submit: Certified copy of ID Proof of registration at a tertiary institution Reference letter from a lecturer or teacher Short biography (max 300 words) Motivational letter Full programme details Link to an unlisted YouTube audition video How to Apply Click here to apply for Fine Music Radio (FMR) Bursary 2026: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Important Notes Ensure your YouTube audition video meets all technical requirements Late applications will not be accepted Applicants must follow all competition rules

raw_data.description_full: Sappi invites South African students to apply for the Engineering Bursary Programme 2027. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 30 September 2026 Location: South Africa (Various Universities) About the Bursary The Sappi Engineering Bursary is designed to support students pursuing careers in engineering and forestry science. The bursary covers essential study costs and provides additional opportunities such as vacation work and entry into Sappi’s Engineer-in-Training programme after successful completion. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Fields Covered Sappi bursaries are awarded for the following qualifications: Chemical Engineering (BSc / BEng) Civil Engineering (BSc / BEng) Electrical Engineering (BSc / BEng) Industrial Engineering (BSc / BEng) Mechanical Engineering (BSc / BEng) Mechatronics Engineering (BSc / BEng) Forestry and Wood Science (BSc) Bursary Benefits The bursary may cover: Tuition fees Study books Accommodation Living allowance Note: The bursary value depends on the student’s household income. Additional Benefits Vacation work opportunities (July & December) Exposure to the Sappi working environment Opportunity to join the Sappi Engineer-in-Training Programme after graduation Minimum Requirements Applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be a South African citizen Be between 17 and 25 years old Be in Matric or have completed Matric Achieve: 70% in Mathematics (not Maths Literacy) 70% in Physical Science 60% in English Intend to study full-time at a Sappi-supported university Selection Preference Preference will be given to: Students from communities near Sappi operations Applicants who meet or exceed academic requirements How to Apply Click here to apply for Sappi Engineering Bursary 2027: Important Notes Applications must be submitted before the closing date Ensure all required documents are uploaded Late applications will not be considered

raw_data.description_full: The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Graduate Internship Programme 2027. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Application Closing Date: 30 April 2026 Programme Location: Pretoria & Johannesburg, South Africa Programme Overview The SARB Graduate Development Programme is a one-year programme designed to develop highly skilled professionals in central banking and financial services. Successful candidates will join the SARB from February 2027 to January 2028 , where they will gain practical experience, attend structured training, and contribute to real projects within various departments. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); What the Programme Offers Graduates will benefit from: Exposure to different SARB departments Training through the SARB Academy Development of professional and personal skills such as: Communication and public speaking Presentation and reporting skills Teamwork and workplace etiquette Hands-on experience in central banking functions Areas of Exposure Participants may be placed in departments such as: Monetary Policy Financial Stability Prudential Regulation Financial Markets Research and Statistics Payments and Settlements Financial Surveillance Currency Management (Banknotes and Coin) Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be a South African citizen Have completed an Honours degree (NQF Level 8) Achieve a minimum average of 65% in final year Have a strong interest in financial services or policymaking Demonstrate academic excellence and strong communication skills Fields of Study Applicants must have a postgraduate qualification in one of the following: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Economics / Econometrics Statistics / Mathematics Accounting / Auditing Law Financial Engineering / Financial Mathematics Information Technology / Digital Technology Risk Management Actuarial Science Fintech Business Analysis / Investment Management Career Opportunities After the Programme Top-performing graduates may be: Offered permanent employment at SARB Placed in key departments contributing to: Monetary policy Financial stability Banking supervision Financial markets Why Apply? This programme offers: Opportunity to work at South Africa’s central bank Advanced training in economics and finance Exposure to high-level policy and financial systems Strong career growth opportunities How to Apply Click here to apply for SARB Graduate Development Programme 2027

raw_data.description_full: Tirisano Construction Fund invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 5 May 2026 Bursary Location: South African Universities Bursary Overview The Tirisano Construction Fund Bursary 2026 supports students pursuing careers in the construction and built environment sector . This bursary is administered by the Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) and aims to assist financially deserving students while promoting skills development in critical infrastructure fields. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Fields of Study Covered Bursaries are awarded for the following qualifications: Architecture Building Sciences Civil Engineering Chemical Engineering Electrical Engineering Mechanical Engineering Construction Management Construction Project Management Construction Studies Construction Information Systems & Informatics Quantity Surveying Property Valuation Urban & Regional Planning Geomatics Land Surveying Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a South African citizen Be under 35 years old Have completed Matric Achieve a minimum APS score of 42 Be studying full-time in an approved field Be enrolled at a recognised South African institution Have a minimum 65% academic average Household income must be below R650,000 per year 👉 Preference is given to students from disadvantaged backgrounds, rural areas, and under-resourced schools What the Bursary Covers While specific benefits may vary, bursaries typically assist with: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Tuition fees Study-related expenses Financial support based on need Required Documents Applicants must submit: Certified copy of ID Matric certificate Latest academic results Motivational letter (max 2 pages) Proof of household income: Payslips / affidavit (if unemployed) Proof of business ownership (if self-employed) Supporting documents (if applicable): Death certificate (if parent deceased) Divorce certificate or affidavit Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be contacted via email or phone If you do not receive feedback, consider your application unsuccessful How to Apply Click here to apply for Tirisano Construction Fund Bursary 2026 Contact Details For enquiries, contact the IDC: 📧 Email: sabelog@idc.co.za 📞 Tel: 011 269 3517 Important Notes Applications close on 5 May 2026 Late or incomplete applications will not be considered Ensure all required documents are uploaded Why Apply for This Bursary? Financial support for high-demand construction careers Opportunity to study in critical infrastructure fields Support from a major development finance institution Improved career opportunities after graduation

raw_data.description_full: The Gauteng Office of the Premier invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Internship Programme 2026–2028. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 8 May 2026 Internship Location: Johannesburg, Gauteng, South Africa Internship Overview The Gauteng Provincial Government, through the Office of the Premier, is offering 20 internship opportunities under the Nasi iSpani Programme . This is a 24-month graduate internship aimed at equipping young professionals with practical experience in data analysis and business process improvement within government . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Details Programme Name: Nasi iSpani Internship Programme Duration: 24 Months (2026–2028) Number of Posts: 20 Stipend: R94,324.68 per annum Department: Office of the Premier Available Internship Positions 1. Data Analysis Interns (X10 Posts) Reference Number: REFS/048447 Fields of Study: Information Technology Data Science Statistics Business Intelligence Applied Mathematics Computer Science / Engineering Econometrics 👉 Ideal for graduates with strong analytical and data interpretation skills 2. Business Process Analysts Interns (X10 Posts) Reference Number: REFS/048448 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Fields of Study: Business Information Technology Information Systems Operations Management Systems Engineering Business Administration Business Process Management 👉 Focuses on improving efficiency and workflow within government Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be South African citizens Be residents of Gauteng Province Hold an NQF Level 6–9 qualification in a relevant field Be unemployed Not have participated in a government internship before What You Will Gain Practical experience in government systems and operations Exposure to data analysis and process optimisation Skills in reporting, planning, and administration Professional mentoring and workplace development Improved employability in both public and private sectors Required Documents Applicants must submit: Completed Z83 application form Updated CV Certified copies of qualifications Academic transcripts Certified copy of ID How to Apply Click here to apply for Gauteng Office of the Premier Internship Programme 2026–2028 Important Notes Applications must be submitted online only No email, fax, or hand-delivered applications will be accepted Late applications will not be considered If no feedback is received within 3 months, consider your application unsuccessful Why Apply for This Internship? Work within the Gauteng Provincial Government Gain high-level experience in data and systems analysis Build a strong professional network Develop critical skills for public sector careers

raw_data.description_full: The Department of Trade, Industry and Competition (the dtic) is offering a 24-month internship programme running from 01 July 2026 to 30 June 2028 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 06 May 2026 Internship Location: Sunnyside, Pretoria, South Africa Internship Overview This programme is designed to equip graduates with practical workplace experience, skills, and knowledge to enhance their employability in the public sector and beyond. Successful candidates will gain exposure to government systems while contributing to building a dynamic, inclusive, and globally competitive South African economy. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Stipend Monthly Stipend: R7 860.50 Stipend may vary depending on the highest qualification at the time of appointment Fields of Study Applicants must hold a tertiary qualification (NQF Level 6 and above) in one of the following fields: Public Management / Public Administration Business Management / Business Administration Human Resource Management / Development Communication / Public Relations Law Software Development Economics Statistics / Econometrics Agricultural Economics / Forestry Science Development Studies Accounting / Financial Management / Cost and Management Accounting Risk Management Internal Auditing / Auditing Natural Sciences (Microbiology, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Bioengineering) Mining Engineering / Metallurgy Commerce Occupational Health and Safety Supply Chain Management / Procurement International Politics Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must: Be an unemployed South African graduate Have a qualification at NQF Level 6 or higher Possess strong interpersonal and communication skills Demonstrate professionalism and integrity Be analytical with attention to detail Have the ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines Show willingness to learn and understand government systems and processes How to Apply Applications can be submitted through the following methods: Email: intern2026@thedtic.gov.za Use the field of study as a reference in the subject line (e.g. Intern2026 – Public Management ) Post: The Director: Human Resources Management Private Bag X84 Pretoria, 0001 Hand Delivery: dtic Campus Corner of Meintjies and Robert Sobukwe Streets Sunnyside, Pretoria Important Application Notes Applications must include: Completed and signed new Z83 Form (available at www.gov.za/documents ) A detailed CV including final year modules/subjects Do not submit certified documents initially (only shortlisted candidates will be required to submit them) Late or incomplete applications will not be considered Foreign qualifications must be evaluated by SAQA If you do not receive feedback within 3 months , consider your application unsuccessful Enquiries For more information, contact: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Donald Mathavha: (012) 394 3864 / 064 850 9379 Teaman Nghunyule: (012) 394 1563 / 060 998 0775 Ursula Pitse: (012) 394 1852 Vusi Mazibuko: (012) 394 5608 Employment Equity The dtic is committed to employment equity. Preference will be given to African males and persons with disabilities , in line with the department’s EE targets.

raw_data.company: City of Johannesburg Executive Director’s Office
raw_data.description_full: The City of Johannesburg invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Executive Director’s Office Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 08 May 2026 Internship Location: 222 Smit Street, Braamfontein, Johannesburg, South Africa Internship Overview The City of Johannesburg (CoJ), through its Human Settlements Department , is offering an 18-month internship programme for graduates seeking practical experience in public administration and municipal operations. This opportunity allows interns to gain exposure to strategic planning, administration, research, and government processes within the Executive Director’s Office. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Details Position Title: Executive Director’s Office Intern Reference: Circular 008/2026 Duration: 18 Months Stipend: R10 104.26 per month Department: Human Settlements Key Responsibilities Interns will: Provide administrative and support services Assist with planning and implementation of programmes Perform data capturing, filing, and office administration Support event planning and coordination Assist with marketing and communication tasks Handle customer queries and service delivery support Contribute to reports, business plans, and strategic documents Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: Grade 12 (Matric) National Diploma or Degree (NQF Level 6/7) in: Business Management Business Administration Public Administration / Public Management Journalism / Media 👉 No prior work experience is required Skills and Competencies Strong communication (written and verbal) Computer literacy (MS Office, especially Excel) Research and analytical skills Ability to work independently and in a team Planning and organisational skills Attention to detail Understanding of public service systems Core Competencies Knowledge of municipal processes Customer service and Batho Pele principles Professionalism and ethics Teamwork and integrity How to Apply Click here to apply for City of Johannesburg Executive Director’s Office Internships 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Important Notes Apply as early as possible (no closing date specified) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Ensure your qualifications match the requirements Why Apply for This Internship? Gain experience in local government Develop administrative and strategic planning skills Build a strong foundation for public sector careers Earn a competitive monthly stipend

raw_data.description_full: Goldwave Construction invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Millwright Apprentice Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 27 April 2026 Apprenticeship Location: Gauteng, South Africa Apprenticeship Overview Goldwave Construction is offering a Millwright Apprenticeship opportunity for young individuals who want to build a career in mechanical and electrical engineering . This programme provides hands-on training in installation, maintenance, and repair of machinery, helping candidates develop into skilled technical professionals. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must have: Technical Matric or N2–N6 in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering Fluency in English Strong mechanical and electrical aptitude Basic welding knowledge (advantageous) Willingness to undergo extensive training 👉 Advantage: Valid driver’s licence or working towards obtaining one Key Responsibilities During the apprenticeship, you will: Assist with installation of machinery and equipment Support maintenance and repair tasks Work alongside experienced technicians Follow safety procedures and standards Participate in on-the-job training Skills and Competencies Good communication skills Ability to work in a team Attention to detail Safety-conscious mindset Willingness to learn About Goldwave Construction Goldwave Construction operates within the construction and electrical sector in South Africa , focusing on infrastructure, engineering services, and project-based work. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); How to Apply 📧 Send your CV to: careers@goldwavegroup.co.za Important Notes Submit your application before 27 April 2026 Ensure your CV highlights your technical qualifications Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Why Apply for This Apprenticeship? Gain practical engineering experience Learn from industry professionals Build skills in high-demand technical fields Start a career as a qualified artisan

raw_data.description_full: The Department of Public Service and Administration has released Public Service Vacancy Circular 14 of 2026 on 24 April 2026 , featuring new government job opportunities across multiple national and provincial departments in South Africa. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 Overview Posting Date: 24 April 2026 Circular Name: Public Service Vacancy Circular 14 of 2026 Official Document: Click here to view DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 What Is DPSA Circular 14 of 2026? DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 is part of the weekly vacancy circulars published by the Department of Public Service and Administration . These circulars provide a consolidated list of government job vacancies available across national departments and provincial administrations. Each department included in the circular advertises its own vacancies, requirements, and application procedures. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); National Departments in Circular 14 of 2026 The following national departments are advertising vacancies in this circular: Agriculture Centre For Public Service Innovation Cooperative Governance Employment And Labour Forestry, Fisheries And The Environment Government Communication And Information System Government Technical Advisory Centre Higher Education And Training National Prosecuting Authority Office Of The Public Service Commission Office Of The Chief Justice Small Business Development Trade, Industry And Competition Transport Treasury Water And Sanitation Provincial Administrations in Circular 14 of 2026 Vacancies are also available in the following provincial administrations: Free State Gauteng Kwazulu Natal Mpumalanga Western Cape (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); How to Use DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 The circular includes an index that allows you to: Click on a department name to view available vacancies Navigate directly to specific sections in the document Identify job opportunities that match your qualifications and experience Each department section provides: Job title and reference number Requirements and qualifications Application instructions Closing dates Important Application Instructions Before applying, take note of the following: Use the new Z83 application form Clearly indicate the reference number of the post Submit your application directly to the relevant department Ensure your application is submitted before the closing date For senior management positions, candidates may be required to complete the SMS pre-entry certificate through the National School of Government. Why You Should Check Circular 14 of 2026 DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 is important for job seekers because it: Provides latest government vacancies in one place Covers a wide range of sectors including administration, legal, finance, and technical fields Includes both national and provincial opportunities Helps you stay updated with weekly public service job openings Final Thoughts If you are actively looking for a government job in South Africa, DPSA Circular 14 of 2026 is a must-check resource this week. With vacancies across key departments and provinces, it offers a wide range of opportunities for qualified candidates. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); 👉 Click here to access the full circular and start applying today: https://www.dpsa.gov.za/dpsa2g/documents/vacancies/2026/PSV%20CIRCULAR%2014%20of%202026.pdf

raw_data.description_full: City of Johannesburg invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Trainee Investigator (EPWP) Vacancy 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Closing Date: 29 April 2026 Location: 48 Ameshoff Street, SAPPI Building, Braamfontein, Johannesburg, South Africa Overview of the Trainee Investigator Role The City of Johannesburg Trainee Investigator position is offered under the Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP) and provides valuable workplace experience in legal investigations and public administration . This opportunity is ideal for candidates with a Law qualification who want to gain exposure in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Complaint investigations Legal research and analysis Report writing Municipal governance processes Contract Duration: Up to 18 months Monthly Stipend: R10 104,26 Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must: Be currently studying or recently completed a Degree in Law Be a resident of the City of Johannesburg Have strong analytical and communication skills Have computer literacy (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Note: Relevant experience will be an added advantage. Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will support the Office of the Ombudsman with: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Complaint intake and assessment Conducting research and investigations Drafting reports and documentation Engaging with stakeholders Ensuring compliance with Ombudsman By-Laws (2023) Supporting awareness and outreach initiatives Skills and Competencies Required Applicants should demonstrate: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent report writing ability Attention to detail Ability to work in a team Professionalism and integrity (Batho Pele principles) Project coordination skills Documents Required Applicants must submit: Updated CV Certified copy of ID Certified copies of qualifications Supporting documents (if applicable) How to Apply Click here to apply for City of Johannesburg Trainee Investigator (EPWP) Vacancy 2026: 👉 https://share.hsforms.com/1cQ6Ywro4TVClVkZ3kG6iEQ469tl Important Notes Only Johannesburg residents will be considered Shortlisted candidates may undergo: Criminal checks Credit checks Qualification verification If you do not receive feedback within 6 weeks after the closing date , consider your application unsuccessful Why You Should Apply Gain real legal investigation experience Work within a government Ombudsman office Build a strong career foundation in law and public service Earn a monthly stipend while gaining experience

raw_data.description_full: Department of Tourism invites qualifying students to apply for the External Bursaries Programme for the 2026 academic year. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 20 March 2026 (Closing Date Extended) Bursary Location: South Africa (Recognised Institutions) Study Levels: Diploma / Degree / Honours / MSc / MTech (Masters) This is a government-funded bursary designed to support students in high-demand and strategic fields aligned to national development priorities. About the Bursary Programme The Department of Tourism External Bursary aims to: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Support financially needy students Develop scarce and critical skills Strengthen governance, infrastructure and digital transformation capacity Build a pipeline of skilled professionals for public sector service Successful applicants will enter into a formal contractual agreement with the Department of Tourism. Fields of Study Covered The bursary is available for students studying in the following disciplines: Construction Management Digital Transformation Business Analytics Graphic Design / Communications Science / Communication Design / Strategic Communication Computer Science / Information Technology / Software Development Law Data Sciences / Data Analytics Development Studies Economics Facility Management / Property Management and Development Forensic Auditing / Internal Auditing / Information Systems Auditing Humanities (Social Sciences) Information and Knowledge Management Industrial / Organisational Psychology Management Services / Operations Management / Work Study / Organisational Development Risk Management Statistics These fields support governance, compliance, digitalisation, auditing, infrastructure management and sector transformation. Duration of the Bursary The bursary covers: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The remainder of the approved study period Annual renewal based on satisfactory academic performance Important: You must pass your modules You must maintain acceptable academic results Continued funding depends on performance Who Should Apply? You should apply if you: Are a South African citizen Are studying in one of the listed fields Demonstrate financial need Have strong academic commitment Intend to contribute to national development This bursary is especially beneficial for students in: Public sector digital transformation Data analytics and governance Infrastructure and property management Risk and compliance Organisational development in government Required Documents Applicants must submit: Fully completed bursary application form Certified copies of academic certificates Academic transcripts Certified copy of South African ID Proof of registration at a recognised institution Proof of parent’s/guardian’s income Motivational letter Incomplete applications may be disqualified. How to Apply DOWNLOAD APPLICATION FORM (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Applications can be submitted via: Hand Delivery Tourism House 17 Trevenna Street Sunnyside 0002 Post Tourism House Private Bag X 424 Pretoria 0001 Email 📧 Bursary2026@tourism.gov.za Ensure documents are clear, properly certified and combined correctly if submitting via email. Important Notes Correspondence will be limited to successful applicants only. If you do not receive feedback, consider your application unsuccessful. Enquiries: Mr Gregory Moroke – (012) 444 6166 Ms Marjorie Sibiya – (012) 444 6174 Why Apply? This bursary provides: Government-funded financial support Multi-year funding (performance-based renewal) Opportunity to align your career with national development priorities Reduced financial stress during your studies If you are studying in a strategic field that supports tourism development, governance, infrastructure or digital transformation, this is an opportunity worth applying for.

raw_data.description_full: Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa (CCBSA) invites South African university students to apply for the Study Buddy Bursary Programme 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 13 March 2026 Bursary Location: South Africa The CCBSA Study Buddy Bursary Programme is designed to support talented South African students who are studying towards science, engineering, and other high-demand qualifications. The bursary provides financial assistance to help students complete their university studies while also gaining industry exposure with one of the country’s leading beverage companies. The programme focuses on developing the next generation of professionals by reducing financial barriers to higher education. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); About the CCBSA Study Buddy Bursary Programme The Study Buddy Bursary aims to support academically strong students who are entering their second or third year of university studies in 2026 . In addition to financial support, selected students may also gain practical industry experience by working at Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa sites during university vacation periods. This opportunity allows students to gain exposure to real workplace environments while building skills relevant to their field of study. What the Bursary Covers The bursary helps students focus on their studies by covering key academic expenses, including: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Tuition fees (full or partial coverage) Accommodation costs Meal allowances Textbooks and academic study materials By covering these essential costs, the bursary reduces financial stress for students and their families. Fields of Study CCBSA prioritises qualifications that are important for the future of the manufacturing and beverage industry. Students studying in the following fields are encouraged to apply: Engineering Degrees Industrial Engineering Mechanical Engineering Mechatronic Engineering Electrical Engineering Chemical Engineering Science Degrees Biochemistry Microbiology Environmental Science Safety Management Chemistry Other Relevant Fields Supply Chain Management Marketing Information Technology (IT) While STEM programmes are prioritised, students in other relevant fields aligned with the company’s operational needs may also be considered. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be a South African citizen Must have a strong academic record , including matric and university results Must have completed their first or second year of university study Must be entering second or third year in 2026 Must be registered at a recognised South African university Must be studying towards a STEM-related or approved qualification Must be available to work at a CCBSA site during university vacation periods The vacation work requirement allows students to gain practical experience and industry exposure. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Process Applicants must submit their applications online and ensure that all required documents are prepared before applying. Documents typically required include: Academic transcripts Proof of university registration South African ID document Updated CV How to Apply Click here to apply for Coca-Cola Beverages SA (CCBSA) Study Buddy Bursary Programme 2026 https://ccbsacareers.co.za/ Closing Date: 13 March 2026 Students are encouraged to apply before the closing date to avoid missing this valuable funding opportunity.

raw_data.description_full: The City of Ekurhuleni invites South African students to apply for the Community Bursary Programme 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 03 April 2026 Bursary Location: City of Ekurhuleni, Gauteng, South Africa The City of Ekurhuleni Community Bursary Programme supports academically deserving students who wish to pursue full-time undergraduate studies in fields that contribute to municipal development and service delivery. The bursary aims to improve access to higher education while developing critical skills needed within the municipality. Students who meet the eligibility requirements are encouraged to apply for funding for the 2026 academic year . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Fields of Study The bursary supports studies in the following fields: Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Mechanical Engineering Town and Regional Planning Architecture Environmental Science Finance Accounting Auditing Information Technology Computer Science Data Science Health Sciences Only qualifications offered by accredited South African universities, universities of technology, and TVET colleges will be considered. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be a South African citizen Must reside within the City of Ekurhuleni (proof required) Must be between 18 and 35 years old Must have completed Grade 12 Must have secured admission or proof of registration for the 2026 academic year Must provide proof of total monthly household income Required Documents Applicants must submit certified copies (not older than 3 months) of the following documents: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); South African ID Matric Certificate Acceptance letter or proof of registration at an accredited institution Proof of residence within the City of Ekurhuleni (not older than 3 months) Motivation letter (maximum 1 page) Proof of household income (salary slips or stamped bank statement with affidavit) Social grant proof (if applicable) Incomplete applications may not be considered. What the Bursary Covers The bursary is renewable annually , depending on the student’s academic performance and the availability of funds. The bursary may cover: Registration fees Tuition fees Prescribed textbooks Accommodation and meals (where applicable) Bursary Conditions Successful applicants must comply with the following conditions: Sign a bursary contract with the City of Ekurhuleni Maintain a minimum yearly academic average of 70% Participate in vacation work within municipal departments when required The municipality reserves the right to withdraw the bursary if a student fails to meet academic or conduct requirements. How to Apply Click here to apply for City of Ekurhuleni Community Bursary Programme 2026 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application forms are available on the City of Ekurhuleni official website . Enquiries For more information, applicants may contact: 011 999 3516 011 999 9230 011 999 1946 011 999 1758 011 999 1844 Closing Date: 03 April 2026

raw_data.description_full: The Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Financial Administrator Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 21 April 2026 Internship Location: Scientia, Pretoria, Gauteng, South Africa Internship Overview The CSIR is offering a Financial Administrator Internship within its Financial Services Support Team under the Advanced Production and Security Division. This opportunity is designed to provide graduates with practical experience in accounting and financial administration. Interns will work closely with management accountants and gain exposure to real-world financial systems, policies, and processes. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will: Gain exposure to CSIR financial policies, procedures, and governance frameworks Capture projects and contracts in financial systems (PeopleSoft) Generate invoices and assist with debtor follow-ups Support financial reporting, including timesheets and expenditure reports Process financial transactions and ensure compliance with regulations Perform account reconciliations and resolve discrepancies Maintain accurate financial records for audit purposes Assist with month-end and year-end financial processes Provide administrative support to finance teams Participate in ad-hoc finance-related tasks Minimum Requirements Qualifications A 3-year Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance Skills and Competencies Basic knowledge of accounting principles and VAT Computer literacy (Microsoft Office and email) Strong administrative and record-keeping skills Analytical and problem-solving abilities Good time management and organisational skills Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) Attention to detail and ability to work in a team Ability to work under pressure Why Apply? This internship offers: Practical experience in a leading research organisation Exposure to financial systems and accounting processes Opportunity to work with experienced finance professionals Enhanced employability in finance and accounting careers How to Apply Click here to apply for CSIR Financial Administrator Internship 2026
